chief_complaint: A 32-year-old female with metastatic thymoma developed fulminant chemotherapy-induced myocarditis following paclitaxel-based chemotherapy.
history: The patient was treated with aggressive multimodal immunosuppressive therapy but progressed to refractory cardiogenic shock and pulseless ventricular tachycardia, necessitating emergent venoarterial extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (VA-ECMO).
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not explicitly stated in the case report.
labs: ECG showed pulseless ventricular tachycardia, and VA-ECMO was initiated. Hemodynamic stability was achieved, and the patient was successfully decannulated after a period of support with signs of improving cardiac function.
